\cos\alpha\cos\beta - \sin\alpha\sin\beta=(\frac{-5}{\sqrt{29}})(\frac{4}{\sqrt{17}}) - (\frac{-2}{\sqrt{29}})(\frac{1}{\sqrt{17}})

Final Answer: \frac{-18\sqrt{493}}{493}

Solution:

Let \alpha and \beta be the angles defined by the inverse trigonometric expressions.

From the reference triangles: \sin\alpha=\frac{-1}{\sqrt{10}}, \cos\alpha=\frac{-3}{\sqrt{10}}, \sin\beta=\frac{4}{\sqrt{20}}, \cos\beta=\frac{2}{\sqrt{20}}.

Using the identity \cos(\alpha\pm\beta)=\cos\alpha\cos\beta\mp\sin\alpha\sin\beta:

\cos\alpha\cos\beta + \sin\alpha\sin\beta=(\frac{-3}{\sqrt{10}})(\frac{2}{\sqrt{20}}) + (\frac{-1}{\sqrt{10}})(\frac{4}{\sqrt{20}})

Final Answer: \frac{-1\sqrt{20}}{20}
